content:
Most free online image tools have a dirty secret: they upload your photos to their servers.

TinyPNG, iLoveIMG, Smallpdf, even Canva — when you "compress" or "convert" an image using these tools, your file travels across the internet to a remote server, gets processed there, and sits in someone else's storage even if only temporarily. For casual photos this might seem fine. But for passport photos, ID scans, medical documents, or anything personal — your image is leaving your device.

I built Firaa to work differently.

How it works

Every single tool on Firaa uses the HTML5 Canvas API and JavaScript to process images locally in your browser. When you upload a photo, it goes into your browser's memory — not a server. The transformation (compression, conversion, resizing, whatever you need) happens on your own device. The download is generated directly in your browser.

Your image never leaves your device. Not temporarily. Not briefly. Not at all.

What tools are available

Firaa now has 47 free tools across several categories:

Compression

Compress JPG to exactly 50KB, 100KB, or 200KB — useful for government portals and job applications that have strict file size limits

PNG Compressor

Reduce image size for email and LinkedIn

Format conversion

JPG ↔ PNG ↔ WebP ↔ GIF ↔ BMP ↔ TIFF ↔ ICO ↔ SVG ↔ AVIF

HEIC to JPG (for iPhone photos that won't open on Windows or Android)

Image to PDF and multiple images to PDF

Resizing for social media

Instagram, LinkedIn, WhatsApp, Facebook, YouTube thumbnails, Twitter X

Passport photo maker for UK, USA, Germany, France, and 20+ countries

Editing and effects

Crop, rotate, flip, mirror

Blur faces and sensitive info (drag to select area)

Add text and watermarks

Black and white, vintage, sepia, sketch, pixelate

Brightness, contrast, saturation adjustment

Sharpen blurry photos

Invert colors

Utilities

Meme generator (no watermark)

Image color picker (HEX, RGB, HSL)

Image to Base64 converter

Circle crop for profile pictures

SVG to PNG, ICO to PNG, PNG to ICO

Why this matters for privacy

GDPR and general privacy awareness has made people more cautious about where their data goes. A tool that processes images locally is a meaningfully different privacy proposition from one that uploads to a server — especially for passport photos, ID documents, and personal images.

Tech stack

Pure JavaScript and HTML5 Canvas API. No frameworks, no server-side processing, no database. Everything runs in the browser using APIs that have been available in every major browser for years.

Try it at firaa.online — no account needed, no watermark on output.
